lintExempt.rationale: Re-affirmed 2026-04-15 (task #22 audit, post-v0.20.0 / post-PR #208). These phase JSONs are shipped historical execution records of PH-01 through PH-04 work; no automated runtime consumer exists (verified: zero grep hits in `server/**`, zero references in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, zero usage in test suites, ac-lint is a pure function library that only runs on caller-supplied plans). The only voluntary consumer is `forge_lint_refresh planPath=<this-file>`, which is an audit-time operation against a specific path and would only re-surface pre-existing F-55/F-56 violations describing already-shipped work. Live drift in newly authored plans is prevented by `npm run lint` (CI-enforced via PR #208) and per-call ac-lint, not by retroactive cleanup of historical records. Original C1-bootstrap "we'll unwind later" framing superseded — exemption retained as the correct end-state for shipped artifacts. Unwind via `grep -l 2026-04-13-c1-bootstrap .ai-workspace/plans/*.json` only if a future runtime consumer is added that would load these files automatically.

stories.title: Define ReplanningNote type with v1.1 retries-exhausted and dep-failed-chain triggers (REQ-10)
stories.description: Promotes the ReplanningNote placeholder type from PH-01 US-01 into a full interface in server/types/coordinate-result.ts. Fields: category ('ac-drift' | 'partial-completion' | 'dependency-satisfied' | 'gap-found' | 'assumption-changed'), severity ('blocking' | 'should-address' | 'informational'), affectedPhases?: string[], affectedStories?: string[], description: string. Mechanical routing rules (no LLM): ac-drift / assumption-changed → master update; partial-completion / dependency-satisfied → phase update; gap-found → defer; blocking → halt. The 5 categories are closed and exhaustive for v1. Adds the v1.1 retries-exhausted trigger (one ReplanningNote per terminal-failed story, category ac-drift, severity blocking, affectedStories: [failedStoryId], description naming the story and stating retry budget exhausted). Adds the v1.1 dep-failed-chain trigger (ONE ReplanningNote per distinct root failed story, category assumption-changed, severity blocking, affectedStories listing the entire transitive closure for that root). Edge case: when both fire (root failed AND its downstream dep-failed chain), both notes are emitted.

acceptanceCriteria.description: ReplanningNote interface declares all 5 categories
acceptanceCriteria.command: grep -nE 'ac-drift' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE 'partial-completion' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE 'dependency-satisfied' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE 'gap-found' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE 'assumption-changed' server/types/coordinate-result.ts

acceptanceCriteria.description: ReplanningNote interface declares all 3 severities
acceptanceCriteria.command: grep -nE '"blocking"' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE '"should-address"' server/types/coordinate-result.ts && grep -nE '"informational"' server/types/coordinate-result.ts

acceptanceCriteria.description: Retries-exhausted trigger: one terminal-failed story → exactly one ReplanningNote with category ac-drift, severity blocking
Multiple terminal-failed stories → multiple retries-exhausted notes (one per failed story, NOT one union note)
Dep-failed-chain trigger: ONE note per distinct root failed story; binary assertion replanningNotes.filter(n => n.description.includes('dep-failed-chain')).length === distinctRootFailedCount
Two independent dep-failed chains: US-01 failed (downstream US-02, US-03) AND US-05 failed (downstream US-06) → exactly TWO dep-failed-chain notes
Edge case: same phase has both retries-exhausted and dep-failed-chain triggers → both notes emitted
TypeScript compilation succeeds

stories.title: Implement collectReplanningNotes with mechanical routing from EscalationReason and EvalReport.verdict (REQ-10)
stories.description: Implements collectReplanningNotes(result) in server/lib/coordinator.ts. Mechanical (no LLM) mapping table: plateau → partial-completion; no-op → gap-found; max-iterations → partial-completion; inconclusive → gap-found; baseline-failed → assumption-changed. Eval verdicts map: FAIL → ac-drift; INCONCLUSIVE → gap-found (rationale: INCONCLUSIVE literally means 'tool could not determine a verdict' = knowledge gap). dependency-satisfied is emitted ONLY by reconcileState (PH03-US-05) when a previously-waiting story is unblocked. Unknown EscalationReason values route to gap-found with severity informational AND a P45 console.error with the exact prefix 'WARNING: unknown EscalationReason routed to gap-found: '. Routing for any blocking note is documented in brief.recommendation; coordinate never auto-invokes forge_plan(update) — the caller decides.

acceptanceCriteria.description: collectReplanningNotes exported from coordinator.ts
EscalationReason → category mapping is mechanical and complete (5 input values, 5 mappings)
FAIL eval verdict maps to ac-drift; INCONCLUSIVE maps to gap-found
Unknown EscalationReason → routed to gap-found AND console.error contains 'WARNING: unknown EscalationReason routed to gap-found: '
Blocking notes appear in brief.recommendation routing summary
Coordinator never auto-invokes forge_plan(update) — verified by absence of forge_plan import in coordinator.ts

stories.title: Implement aggregateStatus with velocity, cost, and optional audit (REQ-11)
stories.description: Implements aggregateStatus(projectPath, options?: {includeAudit?: boolean}) in server/lib/coordinator.ts. Returns per-story status, accumulatedCostUsd (sum of estimatedCostUsd across primary records, null/missing excluded with incompleteData flag per REQ-06), and velocityStoriesPerHour. Velocity formula: velocityStoriesPerHour = completedStoryCount / elapsedHours, where completedStoryCount is the count of distinct storyIds with at least one PRIMARY RunRecord where evalVerdict === 'PASS' within the optional plan-execution window, and elapsedHours = (now - earliestPrimaryRecordTimestampInWindow) / 3_600_000. Edge cases: zero completed stories OR zero elapsed time → velocityStoriesPerHour is 0 (NEVER NaN or Infinity). When options.includeAudit is true, the return additionally contains auditEntries: AuditEntry[] from readAuditEntries (promoted from placeholder in PH-01 US-03 to full implementation here). readAuditEntries reads .forge/audit/*.jsonl with the same graceful-degradation contract as readRunRecords.

acceptanceCriteria.description: aggregateStatus exported with optional includeAudit option
Velocity = 0 when zero completed stories (never NaN/Infinity)
Velocity = 0 when zero elapsed time (never division-by-zero)
Velocity computed only from PASS-verdict primary records (not from generator records)
accumulatedCostUsd excludes null estimatedCostUsd records and sets incompleteData
includeAudit: true → return contains auditEntries array; default false → no auditEntries field
readAuditEntries graceful degradation: corrupt JSONL skipped, console.error called, valid entries returned
readAuditEntries handles missing directory and permission-denied

stories.title: Implement graduateFindings with REQ-12 v1.1 distinct-storyId dedup (REQ-12)
stories.description: Implements graduateFindings(result, options?: {currentPlanStartTimeMs?: number}) in server/lib/coordinator.ts. Returns a wrapper object {findings: Finding[], windowInflationRisk: boolean}. Counts escalation reasons across primary RunRecords for the current plan's story IDs, then DEDUPES BY (storyId, escalationReason) BEFORE applying the ≥3 threshold. Without dedup, a single retry-exhausted story (3 primary records, all escalation 'plateau') would cross the threshold alone and self-graduate — defeating the '≥3 DISTINCT stories = real pattern' intent. Counter is re-derived per call from readRunRecords().filter(r => r.source === 'primary') (stateless, no persistent counter file). Plan-execution window: when currentPlanStartTimeMs is provided, only records with timestamp ≥ that anchor are counted and windowInflationRisk is false; otherwise the window falls back to 'all primary records for stories currently in the plan' and windowInflationRisk is true. Generator records ignored (no evalVerdict). When no escalation reason has count ≥3 → returns {findings: [], windowInflationRisk: <bool>} (never null).

acceptanceCriteria.description: graduateFindings exported with the wrapper return type
Distinct-storyId dedup: 3 primary records with storyId 'US-05' all escalation 'plateau' → findings is empty (count: 1 < 3)
Threshold: 3 records with distinct story IDs all escalation 'plateau' → findings has one entry with count 3
currentPlanStartTimeMs provided → windowInflationRisk false; not provided → windowInflationRisk true
Empty result → returns {findings: [], windowInflationRisk: <bool>} (never null/undefined)
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'graduateFindings.*empty|graduateFindings.*never null' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Generator records are excluded from graduation counting
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'graduateFindings.*generator.*excluded|generator.*not counted' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: graduateFindings does NOT write to any knowledge base — returns the findings only

stories.title: Implement reconcileState with REQ-13 v1.1 failed/dep-failed preservation and dangling-dep rule (REQ-13)
stories.description: Implements reconcileState(plan, projectPath) in server/lib/coordinator.ts. Runs as the FIRST step inside assessPhase, before recoverState. Handles plan mutations between calls. Orphaned record (storyId field does not match any story ID in current plan) → excluded from classification, console.error warning logged. New stories (present in plan AND zero prior primary RunRecords) → initially marked pending, then flow through normal REQ-04 classification. v1.1 REQ-13 preservation guarantees: (a) failed story with unchanged ID continues failed (automatic via re-derivation, no special-case logic); (b) failed story renamed → orphaned-record warning for old ID + new pending story for new ID with retry counter starting at 0 (intentional escape hatch documented in §7); (c) failed story deleted → orphaned-record warning only; (d) dep-failed story remains dep-failed as long as upstream failed story is still in plan, automatically lifts when upstream is replanned away. Dangling-dependency rule: when any story's dependencies array references a story ID that does NOT exist in the plan → classify the downstream as 'pending' with evidence: 'dep <id> missing from plan' and a P45 console.error warning. The story is NOT dep-failed and NOT failed.

acceptanceCriteria.description: reconcileState exported and runs FIRST inside assessPhase (verified by source order or explicit test)
acceptanceCriteria.command: grep -nE 'export.*function reconcileState' server/lib/coordinator.ts && npx vitest run server/lib/coordinator.test.ts -t 'reconcileState.*runs.first|reconcileState.*before.*recoverState' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: Test: orphaned record (storyId not in plan) is excluded and console.error is called
Test: new story (present in plan, zero prior records) → initially pending, flows through REQ-04 classification
Test: full plan replacement (all story IDs differ from prior records) → all old records orphaned, all new stories pending
Test: rename a failed story → old ID becomes orphaned warning + new ID becomes pending with retry counter 0 (intentional fresh budget)
Test: dependency change makes a previously-pending story newly satisfiable → next brief shows it as ready, dependency-satisfied informational note emitted
Test: dep-failed → upstream-replanned-away → downstream classification automatically lifts to pending on next call
Test: dangling dependency (downstream references non-existent dep ID) → downstream is 'pending' with evidence 'dep <id> missing from plan' and P45 console.error
